title: [Bug]:  podman play kube with disabled cgroup
body:
### Issue Description

Under Debian SID, [the podman play kube with disabled cgroup](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4559945404776448/html/int-podman-debian-12-root-host.log.html#t--podman-play-kube-with-disabled-cgroup--1) ([test/e2e/play_kube_test.go:4657](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/eef1b825d5ac4eeb1d739b2e0973b19917c3ea1c/test/e2e/play_kube_test.go#L4657)) test fails under root, and remote scenarios (oddly, [it passes under 'remote'](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6248795265040384/html/int-remote-debian-12-root-host.log.html#t--podman-play-kube-with-disabled-cgroup--1)). Note: The all CI Debian VM's are setup to use runc and CgroupsV1 (not the default).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Generate the test kube YAML and `containers.conf` (see additional info below)
2. `podman --storage-opt vfs.imagestore=/tmp/imagecachedir --root /tmp/podman_test595160758/root --runroot /tmp/podman_test595160758/runroot --runtime runc --conmon /usr/bin/conmon --network-config-dir /etc/containers/networks --network-backend netavark --cgroup-manager systemd --tmpdir /tmp/podman_test595160758 --events-backend file --storage-driver vfs play kube /tmp/podman_test595160758/kube.yaml`

### Describe the results you received

`Error: requested OCI runtime runc is not compatible with NoCgroups: invalid argument`

### Describe the results you expected

```
Pod:
dc664c1a0d4243239b71fa14d38fdedbbebe3759fa928e9803b818eb2b2e7d3f
Container:
1ab66e323ad39ae1183a59b2ffc4a7bea718e6ed83fca542872d4b4751f9aee2
```

```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

The CI test sets up a `containers.conf` with:

```toml
[containers]
ipcns="host"
cgroups="disabled"
```

and `simplePodYaml`:

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: libpod-test
spec:
  containers:
  - image: quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx:latest
    command:
      - sleep
      - "3600"
```

The "remote" flavor of the test [can be observed passing in the raw log](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/6248795265040384/logs/main.log) by searching for the test name.

### Additional information

```

Kernel:  6.1.0-3-cloud-amd64
Cgroups:  tmpfs

slirp4netns-1.2.0-1-amd64
```

comments.body:
I think runc simply has no support for nocgroup feature. cc @giuseppe

The test passes under remote because it does not work correctly under remote, https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/eef1b825d5ac4eeb1d739b2e0973b19917c3ea1c/test/e2e/play_kube_test.go#L4662-L4668
The setenv will not effect the service because the server is stared before the test case.

> The test passes under remote because it does not work correctly under remote,

Meaning, because it can't use a "remote" `containers.conf`?

It needs a
```
if IsRemote() {
	podmanTest.RestartRemoteService()
}
```
after the setenv call to make the server pick it up.

> after the setenv call to make the server pick it up.

Gotcha, and then we'd expect it to fail the same way.  So okay to skip it entirely then if `runc` is being used, or should it key off of `CGv1` instead?
Reading the error message it sounds specific to runc but @giuseppe can confirm.
I think this should only be tested against crun.

> I think this should only be tested against crun.

Thanks for the feedback.  Once @giuseppe confirms, I'll update my skip message and we can close this I think.
yes runc does not support disabling cgroups.  It is a crun-only feature.  We need to skip the test unless we are using crun
